text: The surgeon performs dissection of adhesions and enterolysis in a sixty-five-year-old male patient with a history of multiple laparotomies, focusing on the paramedial hypogastric region during laparoscopic hernia repair.
The surgeon performs primary closure of the abdominal wall defect following the resection of the hernia sac, preparing for the placement of a dual mesh.
During the laparoscopic re-operation for peri-umbilical recurrence, the surgeon dissects multiple adhesions to expose the hernia sac, despite the previous use of a PTFE dual mesh.
The surgeon identifies a three-centimeter defect at the upper edge of the mesh and closes it using non-absorbable stitches, securing the mesh to the fascial margin.
A larger intraperitoneal dual mesh prosthesis is placed to cover the entire abdominal wall.
